_note: Worked example: Copperleaf Mercantile, a fictional omnichannel general-merchandise retailer. Four source systems (TillPoint POS, CartHaven Commerce, ShelfWise Merchandising, LoopRewards Loyalty), store + web sales with a fulfillment state machine, feed-specific raw/staging landings, xref/core customer mastering, warehouse dims/facts, a full nv/bv/mv/BU view stack, cataloged code objects and lineage, a manual merchandising hierarchy override, recon controls, and logged imperfections each aimed at a catcher. Keys starting with '_' are comments stripped before validation.

calendar._note: Retail inverts the B2B week: Saturday is the heaviest day, Mon-Tue the lightest. Month weights carry the Q4 ramp (Nov ~1.4x, Dec ~1.9x) and the soft Jan-Feb trough. Stores close Thanksgiving and Christmas.

vocab._note: Per-category product pools so names, prices, and cost ratios stay mutually coherent, like a real item master.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables.name: supplier
tables.purpose: Merchandise vendors.

tables._note: Category is drawn first; name, list price band, and cost ratio are all conditioned on it. Prices land on retail endings (.99/.49/.00) so the line-amount duplicate mass looks like a real price file.
tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Item master with category-coherent names, retail price endings, and category-tiered cost ratios.

columns.name: list_price
columns._note: Retail price points: category-banded lognormal snapped to .99/.49/.00 endings.

columns.name: cost_ratio
columns._note: Cost ratio by category: grocery/electronics thin margin, apparel/beauty rich. Scalar case branches + ONE expression below — a case whose branches are different expressions silently reuses the first compiled expression (engine caches by column name).

tables._note: Spreadsheet-style merchandising rollup overrides: generated one row per SKU, but only current/approved/eligible rows are applied downstream. The coverage flag leaves ~5% of SKUs unmapped; SCD2 versions, stale_mapping, and conflict flags feed DQ and mapping-review cases.
tables.name: merch_hierarchy_override
tables.purpose: Human-maintained SKU to merchandising hierarchy overrides, used by merchandising marts but not finance marts.

tables._note: 24 stores with deterministic region blocks so fk affinity matching on home_region has full coverage.
tables.name: store
tables.purpose: Physical store locations.

columns.name: opened_at
columns._note: Almost the whole fleet predates the data window; store IDs correlate with opening order.

tables.name: tier
tables.purpose: Loyalty program tier definitions.

tables._note: Loyalty platform is the customer system of record. created_at is sorted with 60% backfill: a tenured book whose IDs correlate with signup date, so in-window volume grows ~6%/yr instead of ramping from zero. loyalty_tier drives both store and web volume via scale_by.
tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Customer master with loyalty segment.

tables.name: promotion
tables.purpose: Promotion calendar; depth tiers match the discount tiers applied on sale lines.

tables._note: Store transactions per customer scale with loyalty tier (platinum ~5.5x of non-members) plus a heavy-tailed per-customer activity weight -> whales. Store assignment uses fk affinity on home_region with a 7% leak (travel shopping). source_stamped supplies ingested_at for the late-TLOG imperfection.
tables.name: transaction
tables.purpose: Store register transactions.
tables.grain: one row per store register transaction

columns.name: txn_ts
columns._note: Calendar-weighted (weekend-heavy, Q4 ramp) and never before the customer signed up.

tables._note: Basket: ~2.4 lines per transaction, quantities mostly 1-2. unit_price is a case on the promo depth flag over the fk_copy'd list price, so discount application is visible line by line and amounts repeat exact price points.
tables.name: transaction_line
tables.purpose: Store transaction lines with promo-tiered pricing.
tables.grain: one row per transaction per line number

columns.name: product_id
columns._note: zipf(0.5): popular SKUs lead but the top item stays ~2-4% of lines.

columns.name: promo_multiplier
columns._note: Scalar case on the promo flag, then one shared pricing expression — promo application stays visible line by line.

tables.name: tender
tables.purpose: Tenders derived from completed transactions; ~8% split across two tenders that sum exactly. Restatement reversals later break the tender-to-transaction recon.
tables.grain: one row per tender applied to a completed transaction

tables._note: Web orders flow through a right-censored fulfillment machine; delivered orders feed the return derivation (web returns run ~16-18% of delivered lines vs ~7% in store). Members shop online disproportionately (scale_by).
tables.name: order_header
tables.purpose: Web order headers.

tables.name: order_line
tables.purpose: Web order lines; smaller baskets, deeper promo participation than store.
tables.grain: one row per web order per line number

tables.name: order_event
tables.purpose: Order fulfillment history emitted by the state machine; the OMS webhook disorder imperfection hits this stream.
tables.grain: one row per order per state entered

tables.name: stock_snapshot
tables.purpose: Periodic perpetual-inventory samples per product per store.
tables.grain: one row per product per sampled store per snapshot date

tables.name: exception_case
tables.purpose: Exception and review queues fed by the controlled imperfections.

tables.name: case_event
tables.purpose: Case status history.
tables.grain: one row per case per state entered

tables.name: mapping_review_case
tables.purpose: Derived mapping-review cases seeded from stale and conflicting merchandising hierarchy overrides.

tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Batch, refresh, and outbound extract job catalog.

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Batch job run history with successful, partial, and failed executions.

tables._note: Execution log for the recon control — run metadata only (no break counts), so it cannot contradict the live break view.
tables.name: control_run
tables.purpose: Daily execution log for the tender-to-transaction reconciliation control.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.6: return line missing original sale line

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations — real lineage from the source rows above.
tables.name: loyalty_member_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly loyalty extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Cleaned customers; normalizes the raw extract's US dates (drift-corrupted dates become NULL and DQ-004 catches the raw rows).

tables.name: pos_tlog_file
tables.purpose: Raw POS transaction log file rows per store batch, including re-polled duplicate batches.

tables.name: pos_tlog_transaction
tables.purpose: Typed and deduplicated POS TLOG rows; re-polled batches keep only the latest ingested row per transaction line.

tables.name: ecom_order_export
tables.purpose: Raw ecommerce order export rows at order-line grain with order, status, and fulfillment timestamps as text.

tables.name: ecom_order_export
tables.purpose: Typed ecommerce order export rows with guarded order and shipped timestamp parsing.

tables.name: merch_item_master_feed
tables.purpose: Raw merchandising item master feed with prices, costs, suppliers, and active flags as text.

tables.name: merch_item_master_feed
tables.purpose: Typed merchandising item master feed with guarded source-updated timestamp parsing.

tables.name: returns_file
tables.purpose: Raw returns file from store service desks and ecommerce RMAs.

tables.name: returns_file
tables.purpose: Typed returns rows with guarded return-date parsing.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Crosswalk of canonical customers to POS, web, and loyalty identifiers.

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Canonical customer joining staging and the crosswalk; pos_customer_ref is NULL where the xref row was lost.

tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Canonical product.

tables.name: return_line
tables.purpose: Returned sale lines derived from delivered web orders (~16-18% of delivered lines) and a 1-in-14 slice of completed store lines (~7%) — channel return economics by construction. ~1% of store return lines deliberately reference a missing sale line (blind returns) for DQ-006.
tables.grain: one row per returned sale line

tables.name: return_header
tables.purpose: Return events aggregated from return lines; store returns carry the store, web returns the return center.

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_customer
tables.purpose: Customer dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_product
tables.purpose: Product dimension.

tables.name: dim_store
tables.purpose: Store dimension.

tables.name: dim_loyalty_tier
tables.purpose: Loyalty tier dimension.

tables.name: dim_channel
tables.purpose: Channel dimension for store, ecommerce demand, and shipped-sales reporting.

tables.name: fact_sales_line
tables.purpose: Unified sale line fact across store and web; margin is NULL for orphaned SKUs (unreportable, as in life).
tables.grain: one row per sale line across store and web channels

tables.name: fact_return_line
tables.purpose: Return line fact.

tables.name: week_end_sales_snapshot
tables.purpose: Materialized week-end sales snapshot populated from business views and refreshed by a cataloged job.

tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed views, target-platform procedures, functions, and extract definitions.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Column/table lineage edges linking code objects to datasets they read or write.

state_machines.name: ecom_order_fulfillment
state_machines._note: Right-censored: orders near the as_of date legitimately sit in placed/allocated/shipped with NULL downstream timestamps. delivered->return_requested feeds the web return derivation, so the web return rate is a lifecycle outcome, not a dice roll.

derivations.name: raw_loyalty_member_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; format_drift later corrupts a slice, which the staging parse turns into NULL signup dates and DQ-004 flags.
sql: insert into raw.loyalty_member_extract
select cast(customer_id as text), first_name || ' ' || last_name, home_region, loyalty_tier, status,
strftime('%m/%d/%Y', created_at), 'BATCH-NIGHTLY-LOYALTY'
from crm.customer;

derivations.name: stg_customer
sql: insert into stg.customer
select cast(member_id as integer), trim(full_name), home_region, lower(loyalty_tier), lower(status),
case when source_signup_date like '__/__/____'
then substr(source_signup_date,7,4) || '-' || substr(source_signup_date,1,2) || '-' || substr(source_signup_date,4,2)
else null end
from raw.loyalty_member_extract;

derivations.name: raw_merch_item_master_feed
sql: insert into raw.merch_item_master_feed
select p.product_id, 'MERCH-ITEM-' || replace(date(p.updated_at), '-', '') || '.csv',
'MERCH-' || replace(date(p.updated_at), '-', ''), p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
s.supplier_code, p.gtin, printf('%.2f', p.list_price), printf('%.2f', p.unit_cost),
case when p.active_flag = 1 then 'Y' else 'N' end, strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', p.updated_at),
case when datetime(p.updated_at, '+2 hours') > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then '2025-12-31 23:59:59' else datetime(p.updated_at, '+2 hours') end
from merch.product p left join merch.supplier s on s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id;

derivations.name: stg_merch_item_master_feed
sql: insert into stg.merch_item_master_feed
select cast(raw_item_id as integer), trim(sku), trim(product_name), lower(trim(category)), supplier_code, gtin,
cast(list_price_text as real), cast(unit_cost_text as real),
case when upper(active_flag_text) in ('Y', 'YES', '1', 'TRUE') then 1 else 0 end,
case when source_updated_text like '____-__-__ __:__:__' then source_updated_text else null end,
file_id, batch_id, ingested_at
from raw.merch_item_master_feed;

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: Three source identities per customer (loyalty members only get the third) — a crosswalk that maps a single system is not a crosswalk.
sql: insert into xref.entity_identifier
select row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,
'TillPoint POS', 'pos_customer', 'POS-' || printf('%06d', customer_id), date(created_at), null, 1
from crm.customer;
insert into xref.entity_identifier
select 200000 + row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,
'CartHaven Commerce', 'web_account', 'WEB-' || printf('%06d', customer_id), date(created_at), null, 1
from crm.customer;
insert into xref.entity_identifier
select 400000 + row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,
'LoopRewards Loyalty', 'member', 'LM-' || printf('%06d', customer_id), date(created_at), null, 1
from crm.customer where loyalty_tier != 'none';

derivations.name: core_customer
sql: insert into core.customer
select s.customer_id, s.full_name, s.home_region, s.loyalty_tier, s.status, s.signup_date,
(select x.source_record_id from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.customer_id and x.source_system = 'TillPoint POS'
and x.active_flag = 1 limit 1)
from stg.customer s;

derivations.name: core_product
sql: insert into core.product
select product_id, sku, product_name, category, list_price, unit_cost, active_flag
from stg.merch_item_master_feed;

derivations.name: raw_pos_tlog_file
derivations._note: Second insert simulates re-polled store batches; staging deduplicates by transaction/line and latest ingested_at.
sql: insert into raw.pos_tlog_file
select l.line_id, 'POS-' || t.store_id || '-' || replace(date(t.txn_ts), '-', '') || '.tlog',
'POS-' || t.store_id || '-' || replace(date(t.txn_ts), '-', ''), cast(t.store_id as text),
cast(t.txn_id as text), t.txn_number, cast(l.line_number as text), cast(t.customer_id as text),
t.loyalty_tier, strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', t.txn_ts), t.status, p.sku,
cast(l.quantity as text), printf('%.2f', l.list_price), printf('%.2f', l.unit_price),
l.promo_depth, printf('%.2f', l.discount_amount), printf('%.2f', l.extended_amount),
t.source_updated_at,
case when datetime(t.ingested_at) > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then '2025-12-31 23:59:59' else datetime(t.ingested_at) end
from pos.transaction_line l join pos.transaction t on t.txn_id = l.txn_id
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = l.product_id;
insert into raw.pos_tlog_file
select 7000000 + l.line_id, 'POS-' || t.store_id || '-' || replace(date(t.txn_ts), '-', '') || '-REPOLL.tlog',
'POS-' || t.store_id || '-' || replace(date(t.txn_ts), '-', '') || '-REPOLL', cast(t.store_id as text),
cast(t.txn_id as text), t.txn_number, cast(l.line_number as text), cast(t.customer_id as text),
t.loyalty_tier, strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', t.txn_ts), t.status, p.sku,
cast(l.quantity as text), printf('%.2f', l.list_price), printf('%.2f', l.unit_price),
l.promo_depth, printf('%.2f', l.discount_amount), printf('%.2f', l.extended_amount),
t.source_updated_at,
case when datetime(t.ingested_at, '+1 day') > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then '2025-12-31 23:59:59' else datetime(t.ingested_at, '+1 day') end
from pos.transaction_line l join pos.transaction t on t.txn_id = l.txn_id
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = l.product_id
where l.line_id % 41 = 0;

derivations.name: stg_pos_tlog_transaction
sql: insert into stg.pos_tlog_transaction
with typed as (
select r.*, p.product_id,
case when txn_ts_text like '____-__-__ __:__:__' then txn_ts_text else null end as parsed_txn_ts,
row_number() over (partition by txn_number, line_number_text order by ingested_at desc, raw_tlog_id desc) as rn
from raw.pos_tlog_file r left join merch.product p on p.sku = r.sku
select raw_tlog_id, cast(txn_id_text as integer), txn_number, cast(line_number_text as integer),
cast(customer_id_text as integer), cast(store_id_text as integer), lower(loyalty_tier),
parsed_txn_ts, date(parsed_txn_ts), lower(status), product_id, sku, cast(quantity_text as integer),
cast(list_price_text as real), cast(unit_price_text as real), promo_depth,
round(cast(quantity_text as integer) * cast(list_price_text as real), 2),
cast(discount_amount_text as real), cast(line_amount_text as real), file_id, batch_id, ingested_at
from typed where rn = 1;

derivations.name: raw_ecom_order_export
sql: insert into raw.ecom_order_export
select l.order_line_id, 'ECOM-ORDER-' || replace(date(o.order_placed_ts), '-', '') || '.csv',
'ECOM-' || replace(date(o.order_placed_ts), '-', ''), cast(o.order_id as text), o.order_number,
cast(l.order_line_id as text), cast(o.customer_id as text), o.loyalty_tier, o.device,
strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', o.order_placed_ts), o.status,
case when o.shipped_at is null then null else strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', o.shipped_at) end,
p.sku, cast(l.quantity as text), printf('%.2f', l.list_price), printf('%.2f', l.unit_price),
l.promo_depth, printf('%.2f', l.discount_amount), printf('%.2f', l.extended_amount),
case when datetime(o.order_placed_ts, '+3 hours') > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then '2025-12-31 23:59:59' else datetime(o.order_placed_ts, '+3 hours') end
from ecom.order_line l join ecom.order_header o on o.order_id = l.order_id
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = l.product_id;

derivations.name: stg_ecom_order_export
sql: insert into stg.ecom_order_export
select cast(order_line_id_text as integer), cast(order_id_text as integer), order_number, cast(customer_id_text as integer),
lower(loyalty_tier), device,
case when order_placed_text like '____-__-__ __:__:__' then order_placed_text else null end,
case when order_placed_text like '____-__-__ __:__:__' then date(order_placed_text) else null end,
lower(status), case when shipped_at_text like '____-__-__ __:__:__' then shipped_at_text else null end,
p.product_id, product_sku, cast(quantity_text as integer), cast(list_price_text as real), cast(unit_price_text as real),
promo_depth, round(cast(quantity_text as integer) * cast(list_price_text as real), 2),
cast(discount_amount_text as real), cast(line_amount_text as real), file_id, batch_id, ingested_at
from raw.ecom_order_export r left join merch.product p on p.sku = r.product_sku;

derivations.name: pos_tender
derivations._note: Single tender for most completed transactions; every 12th splits 60/40 across two tenders that sum exactly. The tender restatement imperfection later breaks the recon for ~0.6% of tenders.
sql: insert into pos.tender
with totals as (
select t.txn_id, round(sum(l.extended_amount), 2) as txn_total
from pos.transaction t join pos.transaction_line l on l.txn_id = t.txn_id
where t.status = 'completed' group by t.txn_id
select row_number() over (order by txn_id), txn_id, 1,
case when txn_id % 100 < 58 then 'card' when txn_id % 100 < 81 then 'cash'
when txn_id % 100 < 92 then 'gift_card' else 'wallet' end,
case when txn_id % 12 = 0 then round(txn_total * 0.6, 2) else txn_total end,
null
from totals;
insert into pos.tender
with totals as (
select t.txn_id, round(sum(l.extended_amount), 2) as txn_total
from pos.transaction t join pos.transaction_line l on l.txn_id = t.txn_id
where t.status = 'completed' group by t.txn_id
select 5000000 + row_number() over (order by txn_id), txn_id, 2,
case when txn_id % 3 = 0 then 'gift_card' when txn_id % 3 = 1 then 'card' else 'cash' end,
round(txn_total - round(txn_total * 0.6, 2), 2), null
from totals where txn_id % 12 = 0;

derivations.name: ret_return_line
derivations._note: Store: 1-in-14 slice of completed store lines (~7%), returned 1-42 days after purchase, window clipped at as_of. Web: lines of orders whose return was received via the state machine, ~83% line participation -> ~16-18% of delivered web lines after right-censoring. Every 97th store return line points at a non-existent sale line (blind return) for DQ-006.
sql: insert into ret.return_line
select l.line_id, 1000000 + t.txn_id, 'store',
case when l.line_id % 97 = 0 then l.line_id + 9000000 else l.line_id end,
l.product_id,
case when l.quantity > 1 and l.line_id % 5 = 0 then l.quantity - 1 else l.quantity end,
round(l.unit_price * (case when l.quantity > 1 and l.line_id % 5 = 0 then l.quantity - 1 else l.quantity end), 2),
case l.line_id % 6 when 0 then 'defective' when 1 then 'wrong_item' when 2 then 'changed_mind'
when 3 then 'better_price_found' when 4 then 'gift_return' else 'fit_or_size' end,
case when l.line_id % 10 < 7 then 'restock' when l.line_id % 10 < 9 then 'salvage_damage' else 'return_to_vendor' end,
date(t.txn_ts, '+' || (1 + (l.line_id * 7) % 42) || ' days')
from pos.transaction_line l join pos.transaction t on t.txn_id = l.txn_id
where t.status = 'completed' and l.line_id % 14 = 0
insert into ret.return_line
select 5000000 + l.order_line_id, o.order_id, 'web', l.order_line_id, l.product_id,
case when l.quantity > 1 and l.order_line_id % 6 = 0 then l.quantity - 1 else l.quantity end,
round(l.unit_price * (case when l.quantity > 1 and l.order_line_id % 6 = 0 then l.quantity - 1 else l.quantity end), 2),
case l.order_line_id % 6 when 0 then 'fit_or_size' when 1 then 'not_as_described' when 2 then 'changed_mind'
when 3 then 'arrived_damaged' when 4 then 'late_delivery' else 'wrong_item' end,
case when l.order_line_id % 10 < 6 then 'restock' when l.order_line_id % 10 < 9 then 'salvage_damage' else 'return_to_vendor' end,
date(o.return_received_at)
from ecom.order_line l join ecom.order_header o on o.order_id = l.order_id
where o.return_received_at is not null and l.order_line_id % 6 != 0;

derivations.name: ret_return_header
sql: insert into ret.return_header
select rl.return_id, 'store', t.customer_id, t.store_id, min(rl.return_date),
case when rl.return_id % 19 = 0 then 'pending_refund' else 'refunded' end,
round(sum(rl.refund_amount), 2), count(*)
from ret.return_line rl join pos.transaction t on t.txn_id = rl.return_id - 1000000
where rl.channel = 'store'
group by rl.return_id, t.customer_id, t.store_id;
insert into ret.return_header
select rl.return_id, 'web', o.customer_id, null, min(rl.return_date),
case when rl.return_id % 19 = 0 then 'pending_refund' else 'refunded' end,
round(sum(rl.refund_amount), 2), count(*)
from ret.return_line rl join ecom.order_header o on o.order_id = rl.return_id
where rl.channel = 'web'
group by rl.return_id, o.customer_id;

derivations.name: raw_returns_file
sql: insert into raw.returns_file
select rl.return_line_id, 'RETURNS-' || replace(rl.return_date, '-', '') || '.csv',
'RET-' || replace(rl.return_date, '-', ''), cast(rl.return_id as text), cast(rl.return_line_id as text),
rl.channel, cast(rl.sale_line_id as text), cast(rh.customer_id as text),
case when rh.store_id is null then null else cast(rh.store_id as text) end, p.sku,
cast(rl.quantity_returned as text), printf('%.2f', rl.refund_amount), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', rl.return_date),
rl.return_reason, rl.disposition,
case when datetime(rl.return_date, '+5 hours') > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then '2025-12-31 23:59:59' else datetime(rl.return_date, '+5 hours') end
from ret.return_line rl join ret.return_header rh on rh.return_id = rl.return_id
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = rl.product_id;

derivations.name: stg_returns_file
sql: insert into stg.returns_file
select cast(return_line_id_text as integer), cast(return_id_text as integer), lower(channel), cast(sale_line_id_text as integer),
case when lower(channel) = 'web' then 5000000 + cast(sale_line_id_text as integer) else cast(sale_line_id_text as integer) end,
cast(customer_id_text as integer), case when store_id_text is null then null else cast(store_id_text as integer) end,
p.product_id, r.sku, cast(quantity_text as integer), cast(refund_amount_text as real),
case when return_date_text like '__/__/____' then substr(return_date_text,7,4) || '-' || substr(return_date_text,1,2) || '-' || substr(return_date_text,4,2) else null end,
return_reason, disposition, file_id, batch_id, ingested_at
from raw.returns_file r left join merch.product p on p.sku = r.sku;

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: insert into wh.dim_date
with recursive d(dt) as (
select date('2024-01-01')
union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end
from d;

derivations.name: wh_dim_customer
sql: insert into wh.dim_customer
select customer_uid, full_name, home_region, loyalty_tier, status from core.customer;

derivations.name: wh_dim_product
sql: insert into wh.dim_product
select product_uid, sku, product_name, category, list_price from core.product;

derivations.name: wh_dim_store
sql: insert into wh.dim_store
select store_id, store_code, region, store_format, case when store_id = 24 then 1 else 0 end, city, state from ops.store;

derivations.name: wh_dim_loyalty_tier
sql: insert into wh.dim_loyalty_tier
select tier_id, tier_name, annual_spend_threshold, points_multiplier from loyal.tier;

derivations.name: wh_dim_channel
sql: insert into wh.dim_channel
select 1, 'store', 'store', 'completed transaction'
union all select 2, 'web', 'ecommerce', 'order demand and shipped sales';

derivations.name: wh_fact_sales_line
derivations._note: Union of staged completed store lines and staged non-cancelled web lines. Orphaned SKUs left-join to NULL cost, so their margin is genuinely unreportable downstream.
sql: insert into wh.fact_sales_line
select s.sale_line_uid, 'store', s.txn_id, s.sale_date, s.customer_id, s.store_id, s.product_id,
s.loyalty_tier, s.promo_depth, s.quantity, s.list_price, s.unit_price,
s.gross_amount, s.discount_amount, s.net_amount,
round(s.quantity * p.unit_cost, 2), round(s.net_amount - s.quantity * p.unit_cost, 2)
from stg.pos_tlog_transaction s
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = s.product_id
where s.status = 'completed' and s.sale_date is not null;
insert into wh.fact_sales_line
select 5000000 + s.order_line_id, 'web', s.order_id, s.order_date, s.customer_id, null, s.product_id,
s.loyalty_tier, s.promo_depth, s.quantity, s.list_price, s.unit_price,
s.gross_amount, s.discount_amount, s.net_amount,
round(s.quantity * p.unit_cost, 2), round(s.net_amount - s.quantity * p.unit_cost, 2)
from stg.ecom_order_export s
left join merch.product p on p.product_id = s.product_id
where s.status not in ('auth_declined', 'cancelled_oos', 'cancelled_by_customer') and s.order_date is not null;

derivations.name: wh_fact_return_line
sql: insert into wh.fact_return_line
select return_line_id, return_id, channel, return_date, original_sale_line_uid, customer_id, store_id,
product_id, quantity_returned, refund_amount, return_reason, disposition
from stg.returns_file where return_date is not null;

derivations.name: nv_sales_line
sql: create view nv_sales_line as
select f.sale_line_uid, f.channel, ch.channel_key, ch.channel_group, f.sale_ref, f.sale_date,
d.year_num, d.month_num, d.weekday_num, d.is_weekend, f.customer_key, c.full_name, c.home_region,
coalesce(nullif(f.loyalty_tier, ''), c.loyalty_tier, 'none') as loyalty_tier,
coalesce(lt.tier_name, 'none') as loyalty_tier_dim, coalesce(lt.points_multiplier, 1.0) as points_multiplier,
f.store_key, coalesce(s.store_code, 'WEB') as store_code, coalesce(s.region, 'Digital') as store_region,
coalesce(s.store_format, 'ecommerce') as store_format, coalesce(s.training_store_flag, 0) as training_store_flag,
f.product_key, p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, f.promo_depth, f.quantity, f.list_price, f.unit_price,
f.gross_amount, f.discount_amount, f.net_amount, f.cost_amount, f.margin_amount
from wh.fact_sales_line f
join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = f.sale_date
join wh.dim_channel ch on ch.channel_name = f.channel
left join wh.dim_customer c on c.customer_key = f.customer_key
left join wh.dim_loyalty_tier lt on lt.tier_name = nullif(f.loyalty_tier, 'none')
left join wh.dim_store s on s.store_key = f.store_key
left join wh.dim_product p on p.product_key = f.product_key;

derivations.name: nv_return_line
sql: create view nv_return_line as
select r.return_line_key, r.return_id, r.channel, ch.channel_key, ch.channel_group, r.return_date,
d.year_num, d.month_num, d.weekday_num, r.original_sale_line_uid, sf.sale_date as original_sale_date,
r.customer_key, c.full_name, c.home_region, coalesce(c.loyalty_tier, 'none') as loyalty_tier,
r.store_key, coalesce(s.store_code, 'WEB') as store_code, coalesce(s.region, 'Digital') as store_region,
coalesce(s.training_store_flag, 0) as training_store_flag, r.product_key, p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
r.quantity_returned, r.refund_amount, r.return_reason, r.disposition
from wh.fact_return_line r
join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = r.return_date
join wh.dim_channel ch on ch.channel_name = r.channel
left join wh.fact_sales_line sf on sf.sale_line_uid = r.original_sale_line_uid
left join wh.dim_customer c on c.customer_key = r.customer_key
left join wh.dim_store s on s.store_key = r.store_key
left join wh.dim_product p on p.product_key = r.product_key;

derivations.name: bv_sales_daily_by_channel
sql: create view bv_sales_daily_by_channel as
select sale_date, channel, channel_group, count(distinct sale_ref) as transactions, sum(quantity) as units,
round(sum(gross_amount), 2) as gross_sales, round(sum(discount_amount), 2) as promo_discount,
round(sum(net_amount), 2) as net_sales,
round(sum(sum(net_amount)) over (partition by channel order by sale_date rows between unbounded preceding and current row), 2) as running_net_sales
from nv_sales_line
group by sale_date, channel, channel_group;

derivations.name: bv_comp_store_sales
sql: create view bv_comp_store_sales as
with monthly as (
select store_key, store_code, store_region, year_num, month_num,
count(distinct sale_ref) as transactions, round(sum(net_amount), 2) as net_sales
from nv_sales_line where channel = 'store' and training_store_flag = 0
group by store_key, store_code, store_region, year_num, month_num
select store_key, store_code, store_region, year_num, month_num, transactions, net_sales,
lag(net_sales) over (partition by store_key, month_num order by year_num) as prior_year_net_sales,
round(net_sales - coalesce(lag(net_sales) over (partition by store_key, month_num order by year_num), 0), 2) as yoy_delta
from monthly;

derivations.name: bv_loyalty_tier_economics
sql: create view bv_loyalty_tier_economics as
with customer_sales as (
select loyalty_tier, customer_key, count(distinct sale_ref) as transactions, sum(quantity) as units,
round(sum(net_amount), 2) as net_sales, round(sum(margin_amount), 2) as margin_amount
from nv_sales_line group by loyalty_tier, customer_key
select loyalty_tier, customer_key, transactions, units, net_sales, margin_amount,
row_number() over (partition by loyalty_tier order by net_sales desc) as tier_spend_rank,
round(net_sales / nullif(sum(net_sales) over (partition by loyalty_tier), 0), 6) as tier_sales_share
from customer_sales;

derivations.name: bv_promo_lift
sql: create view bv_promo_lift as
select channel, promo_depth,
case when promo_depth = 'none' then 'baseline'
when round(sum(discount_amount) / nullif(sum(gross_amount), 0), 4) >= 0.25 then 'deep_discount'
when round(sum(discount_amount) / nullif(sum(gross_amount), 0), 4) >= 0.15 then 'mid_discount'
else 'light_discount' end as promo_bucket,
count(*) as line_count, sum(quantity) as units, round(sum(gross_amount), 2) as gross_sales,
round(sum(discount_amount), 2) as promo_discount, round(sum(net_amount), 2) as net_sales,
round(sum(net_amount) / nullif(sum(quantity), 0), 2) as net_sales_per_unit
from nv_sales_line
group by channel, promo_depth;

derivations.name: mv_week_end_sales_snapshot
sql: insert into mv.week_end_sales_snapshot
with weekly_sales as (
select date(sale_date, '+' || ((6 - cast(strftime('%w', sale_date) as integer) + 7) % 7) || ' days') as week_end_date,
channel, sum(transactions) as transactions, sum(units) as units, round(sum(gross_sales), 2) as gross_sales,
round(sum(promo_discount), 2) as promo_discount, round(sum(net_sales), 2) as net_sales
from bv_sales_daily_by_channel group by 1, channel
), weekly_returns as (
select date(return_date, '+' || ((6 - cast(strftime('%w', return_date) as integer) + 7) % 7) || ' days') as week_end_date,
channel, round(sum(refund_amount), 2) as return_amount
from nv_return_line group by 1, channel
select row_number() over (order by s.week_end_date, s.channel), s.week_end_date, s.channel,
'FY' || strftime('%Y', s.week_end_date) || '-W' || printf('%02d', cast(strftime('%W', s.week_end_date) as integer) + 1),
s.transactions, s.units, s.gross_sales, s.promo_discount, coalesce(r.return_amount, 0),
round(s.net_sales - coalesce(r.return_amount, 0), 2), '2025-12-31 04:20:00'
from weekly_sales s left join weekly_returns r on r.week_end_date = s.week_end_date and r.channel = s.channel;

derivations.name: mart_finance_net_sales
sql: create view mart_finance_net_sales as
with sales as (
select sale_date as business_date, channel, category as product_category,
round(sum(gross_amount), 2) as gross_sales, round(sum(net_amount), 2) as sales_net_amount
from nv_sales_line where training_store_flag = 0 group by sale_date, channel, category
), returns_on_sale_date as (
select original_sale_date as business_date, channel, category as product_category, round(sum(refund_amount), 2) as return_amount
from nv_return_line where original_sale_date is not null and training_store_flag = 0 group by original_sale_date, channel, category
), keys as (
select business_date, channel, product_category from sales
union select business_date, channel, product_category from returns_on_sale_date
select k.business_date, k.channel, k.product_category, coalesce(s.gross_sales, 0) as gross_sales,
coalesce(s.sales_net_amount, 0) as sales_net_amount, coalesce(r.return_amount, 0) as return_amount_on_sale_date,
round(coalesce(s.sales_net_amount, 0) - coalesce(r.return_amount, 0), 2) as finance_net_sales
from keys k left join sales s on s.business_date = k.business_date and s.channel = k.channel and s.product_category = k.product_category
left join returns_on_sale_date r on r.business_date = k.business_date and r.channel = k.channel and r.product_category = k.product_category;

derivations.name: mart_merch_category_sales
sql: create view mart_merch_category_sales as
with active_override as (
select product_id, override_category from (
select product_id, override_category, row_number() over (partition by product_id order by uploaded_at desc, override_id desc) as rn
from manual.merch_hierarchy_override
where current_flag = 1 and effective_end_date is null and approved_flag = 1 and override_eligible_flag = 1
) where rn = 1
), sales as (
select n.sale_date as business_date, n.channel, coalesce(o.override_category, n.category, 'UNMAPPED') as merch_category,
sum(n.quantity) as units_sold, round(sum(n.net_amount), 2) as sales_net_amount
from nv_sales_line n left join active_override o on o.product_id = n.product_key
group by n.sale_date, n.channel, coalesce(o.override_category, n.category, 'UNMAPPED')
), returns_on_return_date as (
select r.return_date as business_date, r.channel, coalesce(o.override_category, r.category, 'UNMAPPED') as merch_category,
sum(r.quantity_returned) as units_returned, round(sum(r.refund_amount), 2) as return_amount
from nv_return_line r left join active_override o on o.product_id = r.product_key
group by r.return_date, r.channel, coalesce(o.override_category, r.category, 'UNMAPPED')
), keys as (
select business_date, channel, merch_category from sales
union select business_date, channel, merch_category from returns_on_return_date
select k.business_date, k.channel, k.merch_category, coalesce(s.units_sold, 0) as units_sold,
coalesce(s.sales_net_amount, 0) as sales_net_amount, coalesce(r.units_returned, 0) as units_returned,
coalesce(r.return_amount, 0) as return_amount_on_return_date,
round(coalesce(s.sales_net_amount, 0) - coalesce(r.return_amount, 0), 2) as merch_net_sales
from keys k left join sales s on s.business_date = k.business_date and s.channel = k.channel and s.merch_category = k.merch_category
left join returns_on_return_date r on r.business_date = k.business_date and r.channel = k.channel and r.merch_category = k.merch_category;

derivations.name: mart_ecom_demand_vs_shipped
sql: create view mart_ecom_demand_vs_shipped as
select n.sale_date as order_date, count(distinct n.sale_ref) as demand_orders, sum(n.quantity) as demand_units,
round(sum(n.net_amount), 2) as demand_net_sales,
count(distinct case when o.shipped_at is not null then n.sale_ref end) as shipped_orders,
round(sum(case when o.shipped_at is not null then n.net_amount else 0 end), 2) as shipped_net_sales
from nv_sales_line n left join ecom.order_header o on o.order_id = n.sale_ref
where n.channel = 'web'
group by n.sale_date;

derivations.name: mart_sales_daily
sql: create view mart_sales_daily as
select sale_date, channel, transactions, units, gross_sales, promo_discount, net_sales
from bv_sales_daily_by_channel;

derivations.name: mart_returns_daily
sql: create view mart_returns_daily as
select return_date, channel, count(distinct return_id) as returns, sum(quantity_returned) as units_returned,
round(sum(refund_amount), 2) as refund_total
from nv_return_line
group by return_date, channel;

derivations.name: mart_promotion_performance
sql: create view mart_promotion_performance as
select channel, promo_depth, line_count, units, gross_sales, promo_discount, net_sales
from bv_promo_lift;

derivations.name: control_recon_tender_to_transaction
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections: tender restatement reversals create the breaks.
sql: create view control_recon_tender_to_transaction as
select lt.txn_id, lt.txn_total, tt.tender_total, round(tt.tender_total - lt.txn_total, 2) as break_amount
from (select l.txn_id, round(sum(l.extended_amount), 2) as txn_total
from pos.transaction_line l join pos.transaction t on t.txn_id = l.txn_id
where t.status = 'completed' group by l.txn_id) lt
join (select txn_id, round(sum(tender_amount), 2) as tender_total
from pos.tender group by txn_id) tt on tt.txn_id = lt.txn_id
where abs(tt.tender_total - lt.txn_total) > 0.01;

derivations.name: control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales
derivations._note: Reconciles intentionally competing BU net sales definitions: finance nets returns on original sale date and excludes store 24; merchandising applies manual hierarchy overrides and nets returns on return date.
sql: create view control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales as
with finance as (
select substr(business_date, 1, 7) as period_month, channel, product_category as category, round(sum(finance_net_sales), 2) as finance_net_sales
from mart_finance_net_sales group by substr(business_date, 1, 7), channel, product_category
), merch as (
select substr(business_date, 1, 7) as period_month, channel, merch_category as category, round(sum(merch_net_sales), 2) as merch_net_sales
from mart_merch_category_sales group by substr(business_date, 1, 7), channel, merch_category
), keys as (
select period_month, channel, category from finance
union select period_month, channel, category from merch
select k.period_month, k.channel, k.category, coalesce(f.finance_net_sales, 0) as finance_net_sales,
coalesce(m.merch_net_sales, 0) as merch_net_sales,
round(coalesce(m.merch_net_sales, 0) - coalesce(f.finance_net_sales, 0), 2) as break_amount,
'mapping_override_or_return_timing' as break_reason
from keys k left join finance f on f.period_month = k.period_month and f.channel = k.channel and f.category = k.category
left join merch m on m.period_month = k.period_month and m.channel = k.channel and m.category = k.category
where abs(coalesce(m.merch_net_sales, 0) - coalesce(f.finance_net_sales, 0)) > 0.01;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: create view dq_rule_result_current as
select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'active customer missing email' as issue, cast(customer_id as text) as entity_id
from crm.customer where status = 'active' and (email is null or email = '')
union all
select 'DQ-002', 'sale line references unknown product', cast(l.line_id as text)
from pos.transaction_line l
where not exists (select 1 from merch.product p where p.product_id = l.product_id)
union all
select 'DQ-003', 'active customer missing POS crosswalk', cast(c.customer_id as text)
from crm.customer c
where c.status = 'active' and not exists (
select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.source_system = 'TillPoint POS' and x.canonical_entity_id = c.customer_id and x.active_flag = 1)
union all
select 'DQ-004', 'unparseable raw loyalty signup date', member_id
from raw.loyalty_member_extract where source_signup_date not like '__/__/____'
union all
select distinct 'DQ-005', 'fulfillment events out of sequence', cast(e1.order_id as text)
from ecom.order_event e1
join ecom.order_event e2 on e2.order_id = e1.order_id and e2.event_seq = e1.event_seq + 1
where e2.entered_at < e1.entered_at
union all
select 'DQ-006', 'return line missing original sale line', cast(rl.return_line_id as text)
from ret.return_line rl
where (rl.channel = 'store' and not exists (select 1 from pos.transaction_line p where p.line_id = rl.sale_line_id))
or (rl.channel = 'web' and not exists (select 1 from ecom.order_line w where w.order_line_id = rl.sale_line_id))
union all
select 'DQ-007', 'stale merchandising hierarchy override', sku
from manual.merch_hierarchy_override
where current_flag = 0 or (effective_end_date is not null and effective_end_date < '2025-12-31')
union all
select 'DQ-008', 'conflicting merchandising hierarchy override re-upload', sku
from manual.merch_hierarchy_override
where current_flag = 1 and conflict_upload_flag = 1;

derivations.name: workflow_mapping_review_case
sql: insert into workflow.mapping_review_case
with candidates as (
select row_number() over (order by rule_code, entity_id) as rn, rule_code, issue, entity_id
from dq_rule_result_current where rule_code in ('DQ-007', 'DQ-008') limit 120
select 900000 + rn, 'MAP-' || printf('%06d', rn), issue, entity_id,
coalesce((select min(override_id) from manual.merch_hierarchy_override m where m.sku = candidates.entity_id), 0),
'open', case when rule_code = 'DQ-008' then 'high' else 'medium' end, 'merch_data_stewards',
'merch_data_steward', datetime('2025-12-15 09:00:00', '+' || rn || ' hours'),
from candidates;

derivations.name: catalog_code_object_register
sql: insert into catalog.code_object
select row_number() over (order by name), name, 'view', 'sql', sql, 'analytics_engineering',
'2024-01-15 09:00:00', '2025-12-20 22:00:00', 'deployed', null, null, 'self-registered from sqlite_master'
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';
insert into catalog.code_object

select 1001, 'proc_nightly_sales_close', 'procedure', 'sql', 'create procedure proc_nightly_sales_close(p_close_date date) begin select business_date, channel, sum(finance_net_sales) as finance_net_sales from mart_finance_net_sales where business_date = p_close_date group by business_date, channel; end', 'finance_data_ops', '2024-02-01 09:00:00', '2025-12-20 21:10:00', 'deployed', '30 03 * * *', null, 'nightly sales close after POS and ecom loads'

union all select 1002, 'proc_rebuild_inventory_snapshot', 'procedure', 'sql', 'create procedure proc_rebuild_inventory_snapshot(p_snapshot_date date) begin select snapshot_date, store_id, product_id, sum(on_hand_units) as on_hand_units from inv_stock_snapshot where snapshot_date = p_snapshot_date group by snapshot_date, store_id, product_id; end', 'inventory_control', '2024-03-01 09:00:00', '2025-12-19 04:15:00', 'deployed', '10 04 * * *', null, 'store inventory snapshot rebuild'

union all select 1003, 'proc_refresh_mv_week_end_sales_snapshot', 'procedure', 'sql', 'create procedure proc_refresh_mv_week_end_sales_snapshot() begin delete from mv_week_end_sales_snapshot; insert into mv_week_end_sales_snapshot select * from mv_week_end_sales_snapshot; end', 'analytics_engineering', '2024-04-01 09:00:00', '2025-12-21 04:20:00', 'deployed', '20 04 * * 0', null, 'modeled refresh procedure for the materialized weekly snapshot'

union all select 1004, 'fn_retail_fiscal_week_454', 'function', 'sql', 'create function fn_retail_fiscal_week_454(p_date date) returns varchar as begin return fiscal year and 4-5-4 week for p_date; end', 'finance_data_ops', '2024-01-05 09:00:00', '2025-10-01 12:00:00', 'deployed', null, null, 'retail 4-5-4 fiscal week helper referenced by close procedures'

union all select 1005, 'extract_supplier_sell_through', 'extract', 'sql', 'select business_date, channel, merch_category, units_sold, merch_net_sales from mart_merch_category_sales where business_date >= :start_date and business_date <= :end_date', 'merch_data_ops', '2024-06-01 09:00:00', '2025-12-20 05:00:00', 'deployed', '00 05 * * 1', 'supplier_sftp', 'CSV supplier sell-through feed'

union all select 1006, 'extract_finance_gl_sales', 'extract', 'sql', 'select business_date, channel, product_category, finance_net_sales from mart_finance_net_sales where business_date = :close_date', 'finance_integrations', '2024-06-15 09:00:00', '2025-12-20 05:10:00', 'deployed', '55 04 * * *', 'LedgerWorks GL', 'daily finance GL sales extract';

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
sql: insert into catalog.lineage_edge
select 1, 'raw_pos_tlog_file', 'stg_pos_tlog_transaction', 'read_write', null, null, 'stg_pos_tlog_transaction', 0.98
union all select 2, 'raw_ecom_order_export', 'stg_ecom_order_export', 'read_write', null, null, 'stg_ecom_order_export', 0.98
union all select 3, 'raw_merch_item_master_feed', 'stg_merch_item_master_feed', 'read_write', null, null, 'stg_merch_item_master_feed', 0.98
union all select 4, 'raw_returns_file', 'stg_returns_file', 'read_write', null, null, 'stg_returns_file', 0.98
union all select 5, 'wh_fact_sales_line', 'nv_sales_line', 'view_dependency', null, null, 'nv_sales_line', 1.0
union all select 6, 'wh_fact_return_line', 'nv_return_line', 'view_dependency', null, null, 'nv_return_line', 1.0
union all select 7, 'nv_sales_line', 'bv_sales_daily_by_channel', 'view_dependency', 'net_amount', 'net_sales', 'bv_sales_daily_by_channel', 1.0
union all select 8, 'bv_sales_daily_by_channel', 'mv_week_end_sales_snapshot', 'materialized_refresh', 'net_sales', 'finance_net_sales', 'proc_refresh_mv_week_end_sales_snapshot', 0.96
union all select 9, 'manual_merch_hierarchy_override', 'mart_merch_category_sales', 'manual_mapping', 'override_category', 'merch_category', 'mart_merch_category_sales', 0.94
union all select 10, 'mart_finance_net_sales', 'control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales', 'control_input', 'finance_net_sales', 'finance_net_sales', 'control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales', 1.0
union all select 11, 'mart_merch_category_sales', 'control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales', 'control_input', 'merch_net_sales', 'merch_net_sales', 'control_recon_finance_merch_net_sales', 1.0
union all select 12, 'mart_merch_category_sales', 'extract_supplier_sell_through', 'extract_output', 'merch_net_sales', 'merch_net_sales', 'extract_supplier_sell_through', 0.95
union all select 13, 'mart_finance_net_sales', 'extract_finance_gl_sales', 'extract_output', 'finance_net_sales', 'finance_net_sales', 'extract_finance_gl_sales', 0.95;

imperfections.name: customer_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Associates hand-key names at in-store signup; typos feed the duplicate review queue.

imperfections.name: duplicate_loyalty_members
imperfections.scenario: Email vs phone signups split identities; MDM duplicate_customer_review queue works these.

imperfections.name: pos_offline_tlog_late
imperfections.scenario: Offline-mode store registers batch up and post 1-3 days late; daily flash vs final sales mismatch.

imperfections.name: orphan_sale_lines
imperfections.scenario: Discontinued SKUs hard-deleted from the item master; DQ-002 flags the lines and their margin is unreportable in the fact.

imperfections.name: pos_price_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Cashier price/tender overrides with supervisor codes; store_ops price_override_audit queue reviews them.

imperfections.name: raw_signup_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Loyalty extract patch pads dates differently; staging parse nulls them and DQ-004 catches the raw rows.

imperfections.name: raw_pos_tlog_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: A POS TLOG parser upgrade pads timestamps for a subset of re-polled store batches; guarded staging parse and DQ review catch the feed.

imperfections.name: missing_pos_xref
imperfections.scenario: Crosswalk rows lost in the 2024 loyalty re-platform; DQ-003 counts unmapped active customers.

imperfections.name: stale_xref_mappings
imperfections.scenario: Expired identifier mappings never re-pointed; data stewards triage.

imperfections.name: stale_merch_hierarchy_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Merchandising keeps stale hierarchy override rows active after a category reorg; DQ-007 and mapping_review_case route them to data stewards.

imperfections.name: conflicting_merch_hierarchy_uploads
imperfections.scenario: A cleanup upload conflicts with an earlier hierarchy file for the same SKU; DQ-008 catches the conflicting category value.

imperfections.name: tender_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Post-void and re-ring corrections land as reversal + restated tender pairs; control_recon_tender_to_transaction breaks on them.

imperfections.name: oms_event_disorder
imperfections.scenario: OMS webhook stream delivers fulfillment events out of order; DQ-005 flags shipped-before-picked sequences.

imperfections.name: customer_email_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Kiosk signups skip email capture; DQ-001 counts active customers without one.

controls.rule_name: Tender totals tie to transaction totals
Finance and merchandising net sales definitions reconcile
controls.expected_break_rate: persistent by category where merchandising overrides or return-date timing differ

dq_rules.condition: every active customer mapped to a POS identifier
event timestamps non-decreasing within an order
return line references an original sale line
current merchandising hierarchy overrides are not stale or expired
a SKU has no conflicting hierarchy re-upload

dataflows.name: customer_mastering
dataflows.sequence: crm.customer (LoopRewards) -> raw.loyalty_member_extract -> stg.customer -> xref.entity_identifier -> core.customer -> wh.dim_customer

dataflows.name: store_sales
dataflows.sequence: pos.transaction/transaction_line -> raw.pos_tlog_file -> stg.pos_tlog_transaction -> wh.fact_sales_line -> nv_sales_line -> bv_sales_daily_by_channel -> mart_finance_net_sales / mart_merch_category_sales

dataflows.name: returns
dataflows.sequence: delivered web orders + completed store line slice -> ret.return_line/return_header -> raw.returns_file -> stg.returns_file -> wh.fact_return_line -> nv_return_line

dataflows.name: code_catalog_and_lineage
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + procedure/extract source -> catalog.code_object -> catalog.lineage_edge -> integration.job/job_run history
